question_text: A student eats a banana that contains 420 kilojoules of chemical energy. During respiration, her body converts this energy into heat and movement. If her body is only 25% efficient at converting food energy into useful work, how much energy is wasted as heat?
explanation: If 25% of energy is converted to useful work, then 75% is wasted as heat. 75% of 420 kJ = 0.75 × 420 = 315 kJ. This shows that most of the energy from food is released as heat energy, which is why we feel warm after eating and exercise.
